differential_diagnoses: Psoriasiform drug eruption with systemic involvement
Atopic dermatitis with secondary superinfection and systemic immune activation
Paraneoplastic erythroderma secondary to underlying malignancy (e.g., lung adenocarcinoma)
DRESS syndrome (Drug Reaction with Eosinophilia and Systemic Symptoms)
next_steps: Peripheral blood flow cytometry for Sézary cell quantification
T-cell receptor (TCR) gene rearrangement studies on skin biopsies and peripheral blood
Core needle biopsy or excisional biopsy of the lung mass and a representative lymph node
PET/CT scan to assess systemic metabolic activity of lymph nodes and pulmonary lesions
Skin biopsy for immunophenotyping (CD3, CD4, CD8, CD7, CD30, CD20)
Systemic infectious workup for the pulmonary nodules (fungal, mycobacterial, and acid-fast bacilli cultures)
Review of peripheral blood smear for abnormal lymphocytes
